records.ety: [OF. encontrer; pref. en- (L. in) + contre against, L. contra. See Counter, adv.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Encountered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Encountering.]

defs: To come against face to face; to meet; to confront, either by chance, suddenly, or deliberately; especially, to meet in opposition or with hostile intent; to engage in conflict with; to oppose; to struggle with; as, to encounter a friend in traveling; two armies encounter each other; to encounter obstacles or difficulties, to encounter strong evidence of a truth.
quotes: Then certain philosophers of the Epicureans, and of the Stoics, encountered him.
I am most fortunate thus accidentally to encounter you.

defs: To meet face to face; to have a meeting; to meet, esp. as enemies; to engage in combat; to fight; as, three armies encountered at Waterloo.
quotes: Perception and judgment, employed in the investigation of all truth, have in the first place to encounter with particulars.

records.ety: [OF. encontre, fr. encontrer. See Encounter, v. t.]

defs: A meeting face to face; a running against; a sudden or incidental meeting; an interview.
quotes: To shun the encounter of the vulgar crowd.

defs: A meeting, with hostile purpose; hence, a combat; a battle; as, a bloody encounter.
quotes: As one for . . . fierce encounters fit
To join their dark encounter in mid-air
